headline: IFQM'S FIRST MSME SYMPOSIUM CALLS FOR A 'NATIONAL QUALITY SPRINT' TO INTEGRATE WITH GLOBAL VALUE CHAINS
summary: The Indian Foundation for Quality Management (IFQM) convened its first MSME Symposium at Anna University, Chennai, bringing together over 250 MSME CEOs, CPOs and CEOs of large companies, quality experts, and academic leaders to deliberate on making India's Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) globally competitive and export-capable. The symposium, themed - India Needs… A Resurgent MSME Sector - called for a 'national quality sprint' to integrate with the global value chains.

content.title: IFQM'S FIRST MSME SYMPOSIUM CALLS FOR A 'NATIONAL QUALITY SPRINT' TO INTEGRATE WITH GLOBAL VALUE CHAINS
content.summary: The Indian Foundation for Quality Management (IFQM) convened its first MSME Symposium at Anna University, Chennai, bringing together over 250 MSME CEOs, CPOs and CEOs of large companies, quality experts, and academic leaders to deliberate on making India's Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) globally competitive and export-capable. The symposium, themed - India Needs… A Resurgent MSME Sector - called for a 'national quality sprint' to integrate with the global value chains.

thumbnail.caption: Venu Srinivasan, Chairman, IFQM, Kavita Sandeep Kaushik, IFQM board member and Director of Quality and Six Sigma, Cummins, Soumitra Bhattacharya, CEO and Director, IFQM, Prof Dr. V Kumaresan, Registrar, i/c Anna University, Malini Gautham from Wilton Weavers, CV Raman from Maruti Suzuki, and Arvind Balaji of Lucas TVS at the inauguration of IFQM MSME Symposium in Chennai.
